macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna: Women's Ritual Purity at Mount Sinai

micro_segments.title: Opening of the Mishna and its three unrelated topics
The case of a woman expelling semen on the third day and her impurity status
The three-day separation and reception of Torah at Mount Sinai
Women's inclusion at Mount Sinai through rabbinic exegesis
The distinction between poletet shichvat zera and other forms of tumah; rabbinic attitude toward sex
Tosafot's explanation of why a woman expelling semen is problematic despite lack of prior Torah law

macro_segments.title: Debate Among Tannaim: When Does Poletet Status End?

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the debate: Rabbi Elazar ben Azariah versus the Rabbanan on day three
Gemara's discussion of textual variants and attributions to different tannaim
Three positions on when poletet ends: day three, day four, or five onot (hours)
Rabbi Elazar ben Azariah: purity on day three
Rabbi Ishmael and the Rabbanan: purity on day four based on Matan Torah narrative
Rabbi Akiva: sixty hours (five complete onot) as the measure of semen viability

macro_segments.title: Reconciling the Positions with the Matan Torah Narrative

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's question: how different positions align with the separation and receipt of Torah
Moshe's instruction at dawn and the counting of onot until Shabbat
The assumption that Moshe instructed on Wednesday morning to receive Torah on Shabbat
Five onot from Wednesday morning to Shabbat morning and Rabbi Akiva's position

macro_segments.title: Technicalities of Mikvah Immersion and Tevul Yom Status

micro_segments.title: Issue of tevul yom: immersion on Friday night and remaining impurity until sunset
Abaye and Hanina: Torah could be given to those with tevul yom status
Distinguishing theoretical possibility from practical reality: did they actually immerse?
Rabbi Yitzchak's reason: avoiding women running to mikvah during Torah reception

macro_segments.title: The Six-Onot Standard and Halakhic Implications for Niddah

micro_segments.title: The Rabbanan's position: six full onot (72 hours) required for semen to lose potency
Comparison with Rambam and Geonim: three onot versus six onot dispute
Practical halakhic consequences: when women may begin counting seven clean days after intercourse

macro_segments.title: Semen from the Man's Body vs. Expelled from the Woman's Body

micro_segments.title: Rav Hisda: distinction between semen expelled from a woman and semen on a garment
Semen on a garment remains impure as long as moist; expelled from woman affected by body chemistry
Rav Pappa's question: semen of a Jewish man in a non-Jewish woman's body
Explanation via body temperature: Jews more concerned with mitzvot have higher internal heat
Unresolved question: effect of body temperature on semen viability in animals versus humans
Prozozdor (external labia): humans have it; animals do not; affects semen potency

topical_tags.term: Matan Torah (receipt of Torah at Mount Sinai)
Tevul Yom (one who immersed, awaiting nightfall)
Women at Mount Sinai; standing at Sinai
